fix: pass large prompts via stdin to avoid ARG_MAX (E2BIG) in copilot provider

When DMTools calls run-agent.sh with cliPrompt, it writes the prompt to a
temp file and passes the path as an argument. For large prompts (~1.5MB),
reading the file into a variable and passing as -p "$PROMPT" exceeds Linux
ARG_MAX (2.5MB total args+env), causing 'Argument list too long' (E2BIG).

Fix: when PROMPT_ARG is a file (always true in CI), redirect stdin from the
file instead of passing the content as a CLI argument. The copilot CLI reads
from stdin when it is not connected to a TTY (non-interactive/CI mode).

1. I record a failure sceanario for EPA Form 4700-4 (.spec.ts)

trained the bot in .md file to use sf242b failure path teamplate)
3. The bot generated the metadata file for field definetion

6. I staged the files
7. I ask the bot the pre-checkin (which I had trained to run the fomat
and lint)
8. The bot fixed the code and ask if I want to dry-run again
9. I re-dry-run after the pre-checkin
10. I checkin the code (Created a draft PR)
11. I use co-pilot in github review (Click "Request review" on my PR
page)
12. The co-pilot suggessed me to re-consider the const name **(I can see
my agent and github co-pilot review didnt talk together)**
13. I accepted the suggessed
14. The work-flow re-run and I got format error **(I can see the
co-pilot review and Suggest me the wrong format -- how to improve
this?)**
15. I use bot (Click "Explain error" button on the job summary page)
16. It explain to Run Prettier with --write to fix.
17. I ask if it can do the fix
18. It ask me to give permission to fix
19. I click "Allow"
20. It ask me if I want to fix in this PR or create new branch from main
21. I tell to fix in this PR
22. It created a new co-pilot PR under my existing PR
23. I approved and merged it to my PR
24. The work-flow re-run again
25. It passed this time
26. I run the test in stg (job: E2E Tests (Deployed Target))
27. It passed the work-flow test in stg

commit.message:
feat(hooks): add pre-commit gitignore guard

Detects staged files that should never be committed (logs, runtime data,
secrets, large files, compiled artefacts, etc.) and automatically:
  1. Adds the specific path to .gitignore (if not already covered)
  2. Unstages the file
  3. Aborts the commit with a clear remediation message

Pattern categories blocked:
  - Log files (*.log, *.log.N)
  - Environment/secrets files (.env, .env.*, secrets.json, config/*.key/pem)
  - Private keys and certificates (*.key, *.pem, *.p12, id_rsa, etc.)
  - Runtime data (data/*.json, data/*.csv, data/*.db)
  - Compiled Python artefacts (__pycache__, *.pyc, *.pyo)
  - Temporary files (*.tmp, *.bak, *.swp)
  - Backup files (backups/, *.backup)
  - Database files (*.db, *.sqlite, *.sqlite3)
  - Node.js dependencies (node_modules/)
  - Python virtual environments (.venv/, venv/, etc.)
  - Large files (>= 2 MB staged blob)
  - Well-known blocked paths (backend.log, config/secrets.json, etc.)

Updated install-hooks.sh to run the guard first in the composite
pre-commit hook (before secret-scan and TODO checks).

Re-run scripts/install-hooks.sh to activate.

🗃️ fix: restrict Onion-Location header to HTML responses only 🗃️

Previously onionLocationMiddleware set the Onion-Location header on
ALL clearnet responses — including SSE streams, JSON API, static
assets, RSS/Atom feeds, and plain-text. This caused two bugs:
1. Tor Browser would abort active SSE search streams when it received
Onion-Location mid-stream and tried to navigate to the .onion URL
2. Non-Tor users saw ERR_CONNECTION_ABORTED because the .onion hostname
doesn't resolve outside of Tor
Fix: Per the Tor Project spec, Onion-Location must only appear on HTML
page responses. Implemented with onionLocationWriter — a ResponseWriter
wrapper that defers setting the header until WriteHeader() is called,
at which point the Content-Type is known. Header is only set when
Content-Type starts with "text/html".
Additional fast-path skips (no wrapping overhead) for:
- /api/* and /static/* and /admin/api/* paths
- Accept: text/event-stream requests (SSE)
- Non-HTML Accept headers (application/json, text/plain, etc.)
- Requests already on .onion (no redirect needed)
onionLocationWriter also implements http.Flusher via Unwrap() so SSE
handlers that reach the writer can still flush normally.
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden exfiltration protection against Shai-Hulud class attacks (#6)

Block GitHub-API-as-exfil attacks (Roadmap Tier 1.5: S-A, S-B, S-C).

The Shai-Hulud attack class uses GitHub's own infrastructure (always
allowlisted) as the exfiltration channel:
  1. Create a repo via POST /user/repos or POST /orgs/{org}/repos
  2. git push stolen data to it (github.com Smart HTTP)
  3. Optionally upload assets to uploads.github.com

S-A — ICAP: block dangerous GitHub REST API endpoints
- Extend the ICAP scanner to also inspect POST/PUT/PATCH to api.github.com
- Always block POST /user/repos and POST /orgs/*/repos
- Block POST /repos/*/releases by default (unlockable via S-C)
- Log blocked attempts with GITHUB-API-BLOCK prefix in exfil.log
- Add acl api_github + adaptation_access rule in squid.conf

S-B — Block uploads.github.com by default
- write_access_rules() prepends an explicit http_access deny for
  uploads.github.com in normal and lock modes (not allow-all)
- Deny is skipped when the releases flag file is present (S-C)
- watch_config includes the flag file in its md5sum hash so Squid
  reconfigures within 5s of toggling

S-C — sandboxed-copilot proxy releases [enable|disable|status]
- Touches/removes /config/allow-github-releases (RELEASES_FLAG_FILE)
- Unlocks uploads.github.com (S-B) and POST /repos/*/releases (S-A)
- Repo creation endpoints remain blocked even when releases are enabled
- Bash, zsh, and fish completions updated

Docs: README security model + capability table updated; AGENTS.md updated;
Roadmap Tier 1.5 marked complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TFLite model loading error on Render

ISSUE: PyCapsule error when loading model on Render:
  'Failed to load TFLite model: CreateWrapperFromFile returned exception'

ROOT CAUSE: tflite-runtime has compatibility issues with certain Python/ARM builds on Render

SOLUTION:
1. Replace tflite-runtime with tensorflow>=2.13.0
   - TensorFlow includes tf.lite for model inference
   - Works reliably across all platforms (CPU, ARM)
   - Slightly larger package but bulletproof compatibility

2. Improve model loading error handling
   - Better error messages to diagnose issues
   - Workaround retry logic for edge cases
   - Comprehensive fallback chain

3. Constrain numpy<2.0.0
   - Ensure compatibility with TensorFlow 2.13

Changes:
- backend/requirements.txt: Replace tflite-runtime with tensorflow>=2.13.0
- backend/serve.py: Enhanced error handling and logging in model loading

Testing:
- TensorFlow 2.12.0 already available on local system
- Model loads successfully with tf.lite.Interpreter
- 122 labels confirmed loaded

Deployment:
- Render will rebuild with new requirements.txt
- Model should load successfully on next deployment
